response: ["Ground yourself using the 5-4-3-2-1 technique: identify 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, and 1 you can taste to anchor yourself back to the present moment.","Place both feet flat on the floor and press them firmly downward while taking slow, deep breaths — this physical grounding helps your nervous system register where your body is in space and can reduce that floating, lost feeling.","If you're in an unfamiliar place, look for a fixed landmark or reference point (a building, a street sign, a familiar object) and orient yourself from there, allowing your brain to rebuild its mental map step by step.","Reach out to someone you trust — call or text a friend or family member and simply describe where you are or what you're experiencing, as hearing a familiar voice can rapidly restore a sense of safety and mental clarity.","Splash cool water on your face or hold a cold object in your hands, as the sudden temperature change activates the dive reflex and helps interrupt the disoriented mental state, bringing you back to a clearer sense of the present."]
